items.title: Automated research cycle completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated research cycle has been completed, reviewing 16 candidates across various sources. All new listings were rejected due to security concerns, while the mainstream watchlist was led by SOL with a score of 55. The report highlights significant risks associated with several candidates, emphasizing the importance of thorough vetting.

items.title: New SolRadar Research Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest SolRadar research cycle report for May 15, 2026, has been successfully published, featuring a comprehensive candidate analysis and detailed diagnostics. Key updates include embedded data source health outputs, watchlist snapshots, and explicit notes on provider degradation. The report ensures all intents are actionable and free from unresolved placeholders.

items.title: New Research Cycle Insights in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new research cycle report for May 15, 2026, detailing the review of 17 candidates from various sources. Key decisions include 10 tokens marked to hold, while 7 were skipped due to concerns such as suspicious activity and low liquidity. The report also notes partial degradation in data transport for certain tokens.

items.title: Scheduled SolRadar Research Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This PR successfully publishes the scheduled SolRadar research cycle report for May 15, 2026, with a fully populated report body and machine-readable intents. Key updates include the completion of the report contract, detailed data source health diagnostics, and explicit decisioning updates based on Grok credibility verdict rules. The report captures candidate decisions and ensures compliance with the cycle's hard rules.
